title: National Committee Update — October 1992
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: October 1992 correspondence and organizing notes from the National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners.

title: Towards a 1992 without Puerto Rican Prisoners of War or Political Prisoners
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: Brochure calling for amnesty and unconditional release of the Puerto Rican POWs and political prisoners and an end to colonialism in Puerto Rico by the 1992 quincentennial. Encourages letter-writing, joining the National Committee, and political education.

title: Petition to the U.N. on P.O.W. Status (FALN 11)
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: Petition arguing that under international law captured Puerto Rican freedom fighters are entitled to prisoner-of-war status and release from U.S. detention. Covers statement of the case, capture and prosecution of the eleven FALN defendants, petitioners' legal claim, and an appendix.

title: Sample Letter to President Bill Clinton (July 1996)
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: July 19, 1996 sample letter calling for unconditional amnesty for the Puerto Rican political prisoners. Addresses Puerto Rico's right to self-determination, UN Special Committee on Decolonization resolutions, and conditions of confinement; includes a message from Barbara Curzi encouraging participation.

title: US Colonialism in Puerto Rico 1898 -
description: Nine-question primer pamphlet on U.S. colonialism in Puerto Rico — island history, strategic importance to the U.S., effects on Puerto Ricans on the island and in the diaspora, the independence movement, and why North Americans should oppose colonialism.

title: The International Campaign for Puerto Rican Prisoners of War
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: Pamphlet outlining the international campaign to free the Puerto Rican POWs and political prisoners, with calls for unconditional amnesty and immediate release plus prisoner mailing addresses for letter-writing.

title: National Conference Against Repression — A Call to Resist
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: Flyer for a national conference against repression and fascism — discussion of U.S. imperialist repression and the development of a national strategy and program against fascism.

title: POW Update — Vol. 1 No. 1
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: Inaugural issue of the POW Update newsletter — Puerto Rican Nationalist Prisoners of War Are Free; Vieques Example of Valor; Contradictions of Andres' Funeral; Free Pablo and Nydia; Lares, Cradle of Our Army.

title: Tribute to Jose E. Lopez — Chicago (October 31, 1999)
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: Event program from the October 31, 1999 Chicago tribute to Jose E. Lopez, honoring decades of Puerto Rican-independence organizing and the campaign to free the POWs.

title: The Shame of America — Psychological Torture U.S. Style: The Case of Alejandrina and Susan (1986)
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs
description: Bilingual (English/Spanish) newsprint article on the cases of political prisoners Alejandrina Torres (FALN) and Susan Rosenberg, documenting conditions of psychological torture in U.S. federal prisons (Lexington High Security Unit).

title: Florence Prison and the Fight to Free Oscar López — Out of Control Audio
description: Audio recording with Lugo Lopez and Alejandro Molina of the National Committee to Free Puerto Rican Political Prisoners and POWs (Chicago) protesting Oscar López Rivera's imprisonment at ADX Florence, where he served 75+ years on seditious-conspiracy charges.

title: Release Report Back — Puerto Rican Prisoners of War (1999)
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: Report to supporters on the September 1999 release of eleven Puerto Rican POWs under conditional clemency from the Clinton administration — Dylcia Pagan, Carmen Valentin, Lucy & Alicia Rodriguez, Elizam Escobar, Adolfo Matos, Luis Rosa, Ricardo Jimenez, Alejandrina Torres, and Alberto Rodriguez. Carlos Alberto Torres, Oscar López Rivera, Juan Segarra Palmer, Antonio Camacho, and Jose Solis were either not offered clemency or rejected the offer.

title: War in America — Stop the Repression of National Liberation Struggles
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican Prisoners of War
description: Flyer announcing a Denver national conference against repression May 28-30; covers U.S. intervention in El Salvador and escalating attacks on Third World communities. Co-sponsors include the National Committee to Free PR POWs, National Committee to Defend New Afrikan Freedom Fighters, National Committee Against Repression, and the New Movement in Solidarity with Puerto Rican Independence and Socialism.
